title: v0.9 HIPRT requirements and feasibility matrix
objective: Grow HIPRT from a narrow Ray3D/Triangle3D preview into a first-class RTDL backend with correctness and performance evidence against Embree, OptiX, and Vulkan.

peer_backend_workloads.risk: 2D segment intersection needs honest HIPRT candidate mapping plus exact refinement.
Polygon representation may require AABB/triangle proxy traversal plus exact containment refinement.
Overlay seed semantics are compositional and may need multiple candidate/refine paths.
Direct 2D lifting may be degenerate because rays and triangles can be coplanar.
Performance includes compile/build unless prepared path is used.
Needs polygon candidate discovery plus exact segment/polygon hit-count semantics.
Needs any-hit row emission without duplicate or false-positive rows.
Nearest segment may be implemented through candidate discovery and exact distance refinement.
Point primitive encoding must preserve radius and k_max behavior.
Likely feasible with point AABB/cube proxies plus exact distance refinement.
Requires deterministic ordering and rank semantics.
Likely feasible but needs bounded candidate strategy and exact rank semantics.
May become ordinary GPU compute unless graph edges can be mapped to meaningful HIPRT traversal.
Intersection-like graph work may be feasible but needs a documented HIPRT lowering.
Requires RT-DB row/predicate spatial encoding and exact predicate checking.
Requires candidate discovery plus deterministic grouping.
Requires exact integer-compatible accumulation and prepared dataset support.

release_rule: v0.9 cannot claim same support as peer backends unless each accepted workload passes HIPRT row-level correctness and has performance data, or has a 3-AI consensus rejection/reclassification recorded in the support matrix.
